title: urtica gracilis subsp. mollis
intro: Urtica gracilis subsp. mollis is a subspecies of the Urtica gracilis plant, commonly known as a type of stinging nettle, a perennial herb native to New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Urtica gracilis subsp. mollis include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, sore muscles, and skin conditions.
